items.title: Average bull price nudges $5,000 at NT's biggest sale

children.content: The Northern Territory's biggest bull sale has recorded one of its strongest results, despite high fuel prices and a big wet season.

gallery.altText: Two brahman bulls, white with a hump neck in view, only one of their heads visible, people in the background holding paper.

thumbnail.altText: Two brahman bulls, white with a hump neck in view, only one of their heads visible, people in the background holding paper.

avatar.alt: A journalist wears a button-up shirt and serious facial expression.

items.title: Aboriginal families in Federal Court bid for traditional ownership recognition

children.content: Two groups of Indigenous families have claimed in the Federal Court they should be recognised as the traditional owners of the NT's Cobourg Peninsula.

image.altText: The coastline of a remote beach.

items.title: This herb could halt a major NT gas project

children.content: The NT government is arguing its planned mega Middle Arm gas industry precinct will be needed to help shore up Australia's future fuel and energy supplies — but it has just identified that a tiny endangered herb could be growing in the way.

image.altText: A small herb plant, green with three leaves growing out of a stone / pebble ground area.

items.title: 'Happy ending for us': These refugee cricketers fled to Australia for safety

children.content: After the Taliban banned women's sport in Afghanistan, most of the women's cricket team ended up relocating to Australia. Now, they're on tour in the Northern Territory — "a happy ending" after a traumatic journey.

gallery.altText: Three women in view, one in centre mid-speech, wearing a pink jersey, black hijab, all standing on a green sports field.
High-angle shot of huddle of Afghan women cricket players, hot-pink shirts, blue caps, standing on green sports field.
Side-on shot of a row of Afghan women cricket athletes standing in hot-pink and blue uniforms, caps and hijabs among them.

thumbnail.altText: Three women in view, one in centre mid-speech, wearing a pink jersey, black hijab, all standing on a green sports field.
High-angle shot of huddle of Afghan women cricket players, hot-pink shirts, blue caps, standing on green sports field.
Side-on shot of a row of Afghan women cricket athletes standing in hot-pink and blue uniforms, caps and hijabs among them.

items.title: Alleged murderer's bail in limbo as Tiwi Islands town erupts in riots

children.content: Lawyers prepare legal action against a Tiwi Islands town manager who blocked an alleged murderer from being granted bail to the islands, where his potential return has already provoked rioting.

image.altText: A photo from a plane shows a remote community sitting on the edge of an island, part of the aircraft's wing is visible.

items.title: Darwin mum's killer ordered to spend indefinite period in custody

children.content: Sheena Fairfield was stabbed to death outside her family home more than three years ago. Her killer will now spend 20 years in supervised custody.

image.altText: A court sketch of a white man, with auburn curly / wavy long hair, scruffed, looking right, blue shirt.

items.title: Legal action filed over decision to clear almost 3,000ha of tropical savanna

children.content: The federal environment minister's decision to wave through plans to bulldoze almost 3,000 hectares — an area 10 times the size of Sydney's CBD — of habitat, home to threatened species, will be challenged in the Federal Court.

image.altText: Murray Watt frowns.

items.title: The surprising city claiming to be Australia's street art capital

children.content: Artists say Darwin has surpassed Melbourne as Australia's street art capital
with work underway on a fresh round of murals across the Top End city.

image.altText: A close up painting of a woman's face in purple, pink, blue and black painted on a building viewed through black fence palings

items.title: A 'horror night' of airstrikes leaves southern Lebanese city fearing what's next

children.content: New waves of missile strikes and vast advisories from the Israeli government to evacuate have left residents in Lebanon's south uncertain of where to turn.

gallery.altText: A car covered in debris from an airstrike in a residential area.

thumbnail.altText: A car covered in debris from an airstrike in a residential area.

items.title: White House asks Americans to report their neighbours as 'aliens'

children.content: The website features a space-inspired background but its real purpose is to highlight the work of ICE.

image.altText: a screenshot of a white house website that reads "they walk among us"

items.title: US prosecutor denies launching criminal investigation into E Jean Carroll

children.content: Chicago's top federal prosecutor has denied his office has launched a criminal investigation into E Jean Carroll, who accused Donald Trump of raping her in the mid-1990s.

image.altText: A woman with blonde hair and orange tinted glasses

items.title: Blue Origin rocket explodes on launch pad during test

children.content: Blue Origin says all personnel are accounted for after experiencing an "anomaly", a term commonly used by rocket companies to describe a launch failure or explosion.

items.title: Erin Patterson mushroom trial appeal set after 'miscarriage of justice' claim

children.content: Erin Patterson's appeal against her triple murder conviction will be heard in August, Victoria's Supreme Court has confirmed, with the director of public prosecutions also challenging the length of her jail term.

image.altText: Erin Patterson being led into a prison van

items.title: Protesters arrested as police enter camp at Olympic stadium site

children.content: Five people were taken into custody and a protest camp was broken up at Victoria Park ahead of construction beginning on the $3.6 billion Olympic stadium development.

image.altText: protester arrested

items.title: Are you allowed to make deepfakes of the prime minister?

children.content: Are you allowed to make deepfakes of the prime minister?
You might have seen AI generated memes of Albanese on social media following the announcement of the federal budget.
Whether or not those are legal comes down to what the post is actually communicating to the people seeing it online.

items.title: AFL backs controversial decision to disallow Hawks' goal against Saints

children.content: A controversial umpiring decision that disallowed Hawthorn star Nick Watson's goal against St Kilda has been backed by the AFL.

image.altText: Nick Watson of the Hawks celebrates a goal, flexing his arm muscles

items.title: The 13-letter winning word at this year's Scripps National Spelling Bee

children.content: Correctly spelling 32 obscure words in just a minute and a half has won 14-year-old Shrey Parikh this year's Scripps National Spelling Bee.

image.altText: A teenage boy holds a trophy on his head, next to an event official

items.title: This comedian has 1.7m followers. He doesn't think it's real

children.content: As a comedian with legions of fans on social media, Will Gibb wishes he didn't still feel the need to legitimise himself. But he does.

gallery.altText: Will Gibb holds a finger to his mouth and gently smiles wearing an orange and maroon crochet shirt against orange backdrop.

thumbnail.altText: Will Gibb holds a finger to his mouth and gently smiles wearing an orange and maroon crochet shirt against orange backdrop.

items.title: Growing garlic is one of life's slower lessons

children.content: In April 2020, I put my first cloves into the ground. Here's what I learnt over the next five years of trying — and often failing — to grow good garlic.

image.altText: Koren holds garlic bulbs in a basket, she's smiling.

avatar.alt: A woman sits in her front garden, which she replanted with native plants to attract local butterflies and insects.

items.title: Our critics can't stop listening to these 10 albums from May

children.content: From long-awaited comebacks to First Nations glam rock and genre-defying polymaths, May was stacked with excellent new releases.

image.altText: Collage of Genesis Owusu, Ecca Vandal, Kneecap and Mulga Bore Hard Rock.

items.title: Farnham says he will not sing on stage again ahead of tribute concert

children.content: Jimmy Barnes, Tina Arena and Human Nature will take the stage to honour Australian legend John Farnham at a Melbourne tribute concert later this year.

items.title: Australians have 'startlingly' low awareness of controllable dementia risk factors

children.content: Too many Australians still see dementia as an inevitability, according to new data, but there is a list of steps you can take to reduce the risks.

image.altText: Jane Turner sits at a desk.

items.title: Queensland prisoner loses appeal to freeze eggs

children.content: The Court of Appeal knocked back the woman's attempt to have her eggs frozen while she serves a ten year prison sentence.

image.altText: Gloved hands hold a tube over a canister which has mist coming out of it.

items.title: Women's shed ex-board member banned over alleged transphobic comments

children.content: A former board member of a women's shed in Victoria has been evicted after allegedly "spewing" discriminatory and hurtful comments about transgender people.

image.altText: a close up of a shed and a sign that says 'Women's Shed Ballarat'

items.title: Mouse plague forces return of COVID-style restrictions at country WA schools

children.content: COVID-style cleaning measures have returned as WA's ongoing mouse plague spreads across multiple country schools.

image.altText: A mouse in a plastic container full of kids dress-up clothes.

items.title: Heatwaves unbearable in UK 'built for a climate that no longer exists'

children.content: As the mercury climbed around the United Kingdom this week and new May maximums were recorded in multiple areas, the once-welcome sunshine is sparking alarm among many people.

gallery.altText: A man lies in the sun, with a large bridge visible behind him.
Several people lying on towels on dry grass, seen from above.

thumbnail.altText: A man lies in the sun, with a large bridge visible behind him.
Several people lying on towels on dry grass, seen from above.

avatar.alt: A woman smiling at the camera.

items.title: Kiwi goalkeeper gets bride's blessing to ditch wedding for World Cup

children.content: Auckland FC goalkeeper Michael Woud has had to put his wedding plans on hold after being selected in New Zealand's squad for the World Cup, but he says taking part in soccer's biggest tournament is not a bad reason to cancel.

image.altText: Goal keeper crouching near the goal post, gesturing to his teammates to move to the right in defence

items.title: Eels reveal Moses injury outlook ahead of Origin II

children.content: Parramatta do not have any concerns with the handling of Mitchell Moses's fitness in the NSW Blues set-up, as the Eels skipper faces a race against the clock to play before Game II of State of Origin.

image.altText: Rugby league player in a blue suit walking through the passage way inside a stadium

items.title: Friday news quiz: A drone drama, a 'dream job' and an old insult making a comeback

children.content: Your hopes of passing might come crashing down like so many drones in a harbour. But it's still worth a try.

gallery.altText: A close up image of a jar full of money, with a 'swear jar' label on the front.

thumbnail.altText: A close up image of a jar full of money, with a 'swear jar' label on the front.

items.title: Mosquitoes can learn to associate insect repellent with food, study finds

children.content: Scientists have found that mosquitoes in the lab can be trained to connect the smell of a widely used repellent with food, but repellents are still the best way to protect yourself.

image.altText: Close up photo of a mosquito on human skin.

items.title: What the fight over London data centre plans tells us about the AI backlash

children.content: Last year in the US alone, projects collectively worth $200 billion were scuppered or delayed as communities protested the construction of new data centres promised to deliver the AI of the future.

image.altText: A man in a vampire outfit lies on the steps of an office building with protest signs against data centres behind him.

items.title: Amazon sued over alleged button battery safety failure in kids backpacks

children.content: The consumer watchdog is suing Amazon alleging it sold children's unicorn backpacks on its online marketplace that failed to comply with mandatory button battery safety warnings.

image.altText: A pink backpack with a white, pink, blue and yellow unicorn.

items.title: Drag queen says Patagonia has launched trademark suit, claiming 'irreparable harm'

children.content: A drag queen and a major outdoor clothing brand have a fight over trademarks. Here's the feud explained.

image.altText: A drag queen with long hair poses in the woods.

items.title: After escaping sexual slavery, Yazidis re-traumatised by return of 'ISIS brides'

children.content: The return of several 'ISIS brides' from Syria has been re-traumatising for Australian Yazidis, many of whom escaped sexual slavery at the hands of the terrorist organisation.

gallery.altText: A woman looks over the Shingal region, Iraq

thumbnail.altText: A woman looks over the Shingal region, Iraq

items.title: NSW coroner absolves police of blame for Pacific Motorway death

children.content: NSW Deputy State Coroner David O'Neil finds police acted within reason during a pursuit that led to a man being killed on the Pacific Motorway, north of Byron Bay.

image.altText: A man stands smiling at the camera with a bird perched on each hand and his shoulder.
